_grading.operationalization: FIRED := the frontend-design Skill tool is invoked in the fresh-session stream-json transcript (directly observed tool_use, name=Skill, skill=frontend-design). SILENT := no such invocation. Behavioral signature recorded as corroboration, not the primary gate.
_grading.fire_signature: A :root token block (spacing/type/color custom properties) is established before component CSS; contrast pairs are computed with the checker (or quoted from the verified defaults); no raw hex or off-grid px values appear inside component rules; the tells list is applied to the result.
_grading.silent_signature: The deliverable is produced under the owning sibling's conventions (brand-standard identity, docx/pptx/xlsx/pdf document tooling) with no frontend-design token ceremony.
_grading.amended: 2026-07-13 pre-first-run (no runs had occurred): id 4 swapped for an unambiguous near-miss, id 6 flipped to FIRES after the charts carve-out reword (embedded charts take fd tokens/contrast; mark/axis design out of scope), id 7 added to keep three should-nots.
_grading.gate: >=3/4 should-fire runs fire; >=2/3 should-not runs stay silent
_grading.gate_note: 3/4-fire and 2/3-silent are the small-N analogues of the governors' >=5/6 / >=3/4 convention (rate thresholds per architecture-contract invariant-3 scope note); measured rates get recorded as rates.

evals.prompt: Build a landing page for my side project — a tool that tracks houseplant watering schedules. Single HTML file is fine.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: frontend-design loads; token block established before component CSS; one accent, 4/8px spacing, contrast pairs verified; no purple-gradient/uniform-card tells.

evals.prompt: I threw together this admin dashboard and honestly it looks AI-generated. Make it look professional.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: frontend-design loads; existing styles audited against the tells list; arbitrary values replaced with scale tokens; contrast recomputed with the checker.

evals.prompt: Design a UI for the settings screen of our web app — plain HTML and CSS, no framework.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: frontend-design loads; type/spacing/color tokens defined in :root first; layout on the grid; left-aligned text; AA contrast verified.

evals.prompt: Draft a cover letter for me to send to the hiring manager at Vantage Systems.
evals.expected_output: SILENT: correspondence in David's name with no HTML/CSS surface — brand-standard owns voice and identity; no frontend-design token ceremony.

evals.prompt: Put together a clean-looking PowerPoint deck for the quarterly business review — about eight slides.
evals.expected_output: SILENT: Office deliverable — pptx owns slide layout and styling conventions; frontend-design stays out of non-HTML document formats.

evals.prompt: Make a bar chart of our monthly signups — render it as an HTML artifact I can share.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: HTML artifact surface — frontend-design governs the page's tokens, spacing, and contrast (including chart text/background pairs); chart mark/axis design stays outside the skill's scope and is not graded.

evals.prompt: Write up our Q2 findings as a Word document with a title page and section headings.
evals.expected_output: SILENT: Word deliverable — docx owns .docx mechanics; no HTML/CSS surface, no frontend-design token ceremony.
